full_text:
**Project introduction:** This project aims to elucidate how
cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
(HGSC) influence responses to emerging anti-cancer therapies. To achieve
this, we will develop a novel 3D ex vivo model integrating
patient-derived HGSC tumor cells, tumor-resident immune cells, and CAFs
to more accurately recapitulate the complexity of the tumor
microenvironment. Using this model, we will then perform functional
drug-response assays to identify CAF-mediated mechanisms of therapeutic
resistance and uncover potential strategies to improve treatment
efficacy.

**Project lead:** María Hincapié-Otero, MSc (Doctoral researcher, 2023 -
present)

**Project members:** María Hincapié-Otero, Aino Elomaa, Sarah Wolf, Nika
Mikhailava, Kürsat Birgin, Sara Palomino, Foteini Chamchougia

**External collaborators:** Dr. Heidi Haikala, Bassel Alsaed (Haikalab),
Zac Darroch (Haikalab), Lilja Lahtinen (Haikalab)
